audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua Shiloh assembly, seven-tribe survey, and Benjamin allotment register, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Shiloh sanctuary, remaining seven tribes, Joshua's rebuke, three-man-per-tribe survey, written land description, lot casting before YHWH, Benjamin boundaries, and Benjamin city lists.

verses.restored: All congregation of children of Israel assembled at Shiloh, and they set up tent of meeting there. Land was subdued before them.
There remained among children of Israel seven tribes whose inheritance they had not divided.
Joshua said to children of Israel, How long will you be slack to go in to possess land YHWH Divine of your fathers gave you?
Provide for yourselves three men for each tribe, and I will send them; they shall rise and walk through land and write it down according to their inheritance, and come to me.
They shall divide it into seven portions. Judah shall stand in its territory on south, and house of Joseph shall stand in their territory on north.
You shall write land in seven portions and bring it to me here, and I will cast lot for you here before YHWH our Divine.

For Levites have no portion in your midst, because priesthood of YHWH is their inheritance; and Gad, Reuben, and half tribe of Manasseh received their inheritance across Jordan eastward, which Moses servant of YHWH gave them.
The men rose and went, and Joshua commanded those going to write land, saying, Go, walk through land, write it down, and return to me; here I will cast lot for you before YHWH at Shiloh.
The men went and passed through land, and wrote it by cities into seven portions in a scroll; they came to Joshua at camp at Shiloh.
Joshua cast lot for them at Shiloh before YHWH, and there Joshua divided land to children of Israel according to their divisions.
Lot of tribe of sons of Benjamin came up according to their clans; territory of their lot went out between sons of Judah and sons of Joseph.
Their border on north side was from Jordan; border went up to shoulder of Jericho northward, went up through hill country westward, and its end was wilderness of Beth-aven.
Border passed from there to Luz, to shoulder of Luz southward, that is Bethel; border went down to Ataroth-addar by mountain south of Lower Beth-horon.
Border was marked and turned on west side southward, from mountain before Beth-horon southward, and its end was at Kiriath-baal, that is Kiriath-jearim, city of sons of Judah. This was west side.
South side was from edge of Kiriath-jearim; border went out westward and went out to spring of waters of Nephtoah.

Border went down to edge of mountain before Valley of son of Hinnom, which is in Valley of Rephaim northward; it went down Valley of Hinnom to shoulder of Jebusite southward and went down to En-rogel.
It was marked northward and went out to En-shemesh, went out to Geliloth opposite ascent of Adummim, and went down to Stone of Bohan son of Reuben.
It passed to shoulder before Arabah northward and went down to Arabah.
Border passed to shoulder of Beth-hoglah northward, and border's end was at north bay of Salt Sea, at south end of Jordan. This was south border.
Jordan bordered it on east side. This was inheritance of sons of Benjamin by its borders around, according to their clans.
Cities of tribe of sons of Benjamin according to their clans were Jericho, Beth-hoglah, Emek-keziz,
Beth-arabah, Zemaraim, Bethel,
Avvim, Parah, Ophrah,
Chephar-ammoni, Ophni, and Geba; twelve cities with their villages.
Gibeon, Ramah, Beeroth,
Mizpeh, Chephirah, Mozah,
Rekem, Irpeel, Taralah,
Zela, Haeleph, Jebusite—that is Jerusalem—Gibeah, and Kiriath; fourteen cities with their villages. This is inheritance of sons of Benjamin according to their clans.

translation_notes: Shiloh becomes the central assembly site and the tent of meeting is established there, shifting the narrative from campaigns to sanctuary-based distribution.
The land being 'subdued' is a victor's administrative statement, not proof that no other communities remained or suffered.
Joshua's rebuke concerns delay in allocation, but land survey and lot-casting still operate within conquest assumptions.
The three-man-per-tribe survey creates written description by cities, showing early administrative textualization of land claims.
Lots are cast before YHWH at Shiloh, but ancient lot practice cannot override modern evidence, consent, land rights, or existing inhabitants.
Benjamin's allotment sits between Judah and Joseph and includes sites that will become politically and narratively important, including Bethel, Gibeon, Ramah, Mizpeh, Gibeah, and Jerusalem/Jebus.
Jerusalem is called Jebusite in the city list, preserving continuing non-Israelite identity inside an allotted space.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 18 to convert sacred lots, surveys, or ancient city lists into modern title claims or displacement tools. Center transparent public process, written accountability, and the text's own witness that allotment and actual inhabited reality are not the same thing.
